entry.title: WINTER WEATHER ADVISORY FOR WEDNESDAY ABOVE 5,000 FEET
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 4:00 a.m. A Winter Weather Advisory is in effect from 4:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. Wednesday above 5,000 feet, for mountain areas in parts of Southern Oregon. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford said snow is expected with accumulations of 7 to 10 inches. Winds gusting as high as 40...

entry.title: SKARLATOS MAKES STATEMENT ON BOYCOTTING GAS TAX VOTE
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:50 a.m. On Monday, State Representative Alek Skarlatos issued a statement following his decision to walk out on a floor session before a vote was taken to move the gas tax referendum from the November ballot to the May Primary Election. Skarlatos said, “Today I left the floor before the vote on...

entry.title: TRACTOR SUPPLY COMPANY PLANS TO OPEN IN MARCH
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:50 a.m. In the KQEN Business Spotlight: Tractor Supply Company plans to open its new store in Roseburg later this month. A TSC spokesperson told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the soft opening is set for March 21st with the grand opening on March 28th. The store will be...

entry.title: 5TH ANNUAL MUSIC AT THE COURTHOUSE CONCERT ON WEDNESDAY
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:40 a.m. Douglas County Commissioners 5th annual Music at the Courthouse Concert will be this Wednesday, March 4th, featuring local schools and music programs. It is open to the public. A county release said music will be presented from 10:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. Bands and choirs from eight Douglas County schools...

entry.title: NEXTDOOR PLATFORMS SAYS HUCREST IS ROSEBURG’ FRIENDLIEST NEIGHBORHOOD
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:30 a.m. Nextdoor Holdings, which operates the Nextdoor Platform, has released its 2026 rankings of the friendliest Roseburg area neighborhoods. A Nextdoor release said the results spotlight the communities where neighbors are most engaged, supportive and welcoming. Data captures everything from neighbors helping each other out to how they share resources and...

entry.title: GREATEST OF THE GRAPE HAPPENING THIS SATURDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:20 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: Oregon’s oldest wine celebration is happening this Saturday. The Greatest of the Grape is presented by the Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Association. It will take place Saturday night from 6:00 p.m. to 9:0 pm. at Seven Feathers Casino Resort in Canyonville. The 55th annual event features...

entry.title: ROSEBURG CONCERT CHORALE AND UMPQUA CHAMBER ORCHESTRA IN CONCERT
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:05 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: Umpqua Community College Performing and Visual Arts presents the Roseburg Concert Chorale and Umpqua Chamber Orchestra in concert on Friday, March 13th at 7:00 p.m. and Sunday, March 15th at 3:00 p.m. in the Whipple Fine Arts Center on the UCC campus. The concert will...

entry.title: ELKTON MAN JAILED AFTER BEING FOUND INSIDE A SHOP
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 2:50 a.m. An Elkton man was jailed after being found inside a shop on Sunday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said just after 12:00 p.m. deputies were advised that the 43-year-old man was at the property in the 800 block of Azalea Drive in Elkton. Units arrived and located the suspect...

entry.title: Autism Awareness Walk Draws Crowd in Roseburg Amid Fremont Middle School Case
entry.summary: Roseburg community members gathered Sunday for an Autism Awareness Solidarity Walk for Justice, marching from Fir Grove Park to Fremont Middle School after the arrests of three school employees tied to the Developmental Learning Center.

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.02.26
entry.summary: Live from Salem, State Representative Court Boice with an update on legislation as the short session is in its final days.

entry.title: Man Arrested After Reported Stick Assault At Stewart Park
entry.summary: Man arrested after police said a man sleeping under the Stewart Park pavilion was struck in the head with a large stick. Officers reported the victim suffered a laceration and was transported to Mercy Medical Center. The suspect fled, was tased and lodged at corrections.

entry.title: Man presumed drowned after South Umpqua River rescue attempt
entry.summary: ROSEBURG, Ore. - A man is presumed drowned after an emergency incident in the South Umpqua River on Friday.

entry.title: Police Log: February 27 - March 2, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported arrests and citations Feb. 27–Mar. 2, including multiple DUII arrests, a Stewart Park assault, theft and trespass cases, warrants, and probation/parole violations, according to City of Roseburg Police Logs.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE CHARGE MAN WITH HARASSMENT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 11:00 a.m. Roseburg Police charged a man with harassment on Sunday afternoon. An RPD report said at 2:40 p.m. officers responded to the 1000 block of Southeast Main Street after a caller said there had been a disturbance. During the investigation, it was determined that the 68-year-old had bit a victim on...

entry.title: Man Presumed Drowned After Being Swept Downstream In South Umpqua River
entry.summary: Man presumed drowned after a reported river rescue Friday morning on the South Umpqua River near Portland Avenue. Call came in about 7:23 a.m. Deputies and partner agencies searched for hours. Additional search efforts will continue.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:40 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a warrant on Friday night. An RPD report said just after 10:35 p.m. officers contacted the 40-year-old near a store in the 900 block of Northwest Garden Valley Boulevard, knowing he had the warrant. It was confirmed and the man was taken into...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR PAROLE VIOLATION, METHAMPHETAMINE
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a parole violation and unlawful possession of methamphetamine on Friday. An RPD report said at 12:00 p.m. officers contacted the 50-year-old in the 200 block of Northeast Jackson Street after observing him with an open beer. Officers knew that as a stipulation of his...

entry.title: WOMAN TAKEN TO HOSPITAL AFTER SINGLE-VEHICLE CRASH
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:20 a.m. A woman was taken to the hospital after a single vehicle crash on Sunday night. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at approximately 11:10 p.m. a driver and a passenger were headed downhill on Longs Ranch near the 7000 block of Melqua Road. There was thick fog in the...

entry.title: MAN PRESUMED DROWNED IN THE SOUTH UMPQUA RIVER
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:10 a.m. A man is presumed drowned following an incident in the South Umpqua River on Friday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at approximately 7:20 a.m. 911 dispatchers received a report of a man stranded in the middle of the river, yelling for help. DCSO along with multiple other agencies...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR THEFT INCIDENT AND WARRANT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:00 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a theft incident and a warrant on Friday. An RPD report said at 10:20 a.m. officers responded to the 2100 block of Northeast Diamond Lake Boulevard after the 42-year-old allegedly grabbed an employee before leaving the business. Officers contacted the suspect a short distance...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE ADD MORE SEX CRIME CHARGES TO MAN ALREADY IN JAIL
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 9:40 a.m. Roseburg Police added more sex crime charges on Friday, to a man that was already in jail. An RPD report said additional review of digital evidence related to the original case allegedly revealed multiple new sex crimes involving the same victim. As a result, at 11:30 a.m. 41-year-old Gregory Van...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR REPORTED ASSAULT AT STEWART PARK
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 9:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a reported assault in Stewart Park early Friday morning. An RPD report said shortly after 5:30 a.m. officers contacted a victim who said he had been assaulted by an unknown man at the park, in the 1000 block of Northwest Stewart Parkway. The victim...

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 3.2.26
entry.summary: Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Executive Director Ali Rodgers talks about their Greatest of the Grape event, happening on Saturday at Seven Feathers Casino and Resort.

entry.title: Miss Douglas County Scholarship program crowns new titleholders
entry.summary: WINCHESTER — Rebecca Ngigi said she walked into the Miss Douglas County Scholarship program expecting to wear pretty dresses and dedicate her time to serving the community and as of Sunday she is even more involved.

entry.title: March 2, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: SENATORS RESPOND TO U.S. MILITARY ACTION IN IRAN
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 6:10 a.m. On Saturday, both of Oregon’s U.S. Senators responded to U.S. military action in Iran. Senator Ron Wyden said, “Donald Trump is unilaterally dragging us into another costly and deadly war in the Middle East that the American people don’t want”. Wyden said, “Trump and Hegseth are putting American lives on...

entry.title: ROSEBURG RECEIVES ABOVE NORMAL RAIN FOR FEBRUARY
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:50 a.m. The Umpqua Valley received an above normal amount of rain for February but is still significantly behind the amount of precipitation usually received for this point in the rain year. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the Roseburg Regional...

entry.title: MYRTLE CREEK CITY COUNCIL MEETING MONDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:40 a.m. The Myrtle Creek City Council has a meeting on Monday night at 5:30 p.m. The agenda includes the consideration of approving a three-year intergovernmental agreement between the city and the Myrtle Creek Rural Fire Protection District to begin in the upcoming fiscal year. The council will look at the future...

entry.title: WINSTON CITY COUNCIL MEETING MONDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:30 a.m. The Winston City Council will meet on Monday night. Councilors will consider a resolution to authorize the city manager to enter into an intergovernmental agreement with Douglas County to continue providing law enforcement dispatch services for the upcoming fiscal year. The new contract is increasing by three percent for a...

entry.title: EARLY LITERACY EVENT HAPPENING MARCH 9TH
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:30 a.m. Roseburg Schools will host their third and final early childhood literacy event of the season, next Monday, March 9th, as part of the district’s grant-funded efforts intended to enhance literacy development across the community. A Roseburg Schools release said all Roseburg-area families with children ages 2 to 7 are invited...

entry.title: SPRING INTO GARDENING RETURNS ON SATURDAY
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:05 a.m. The Spring into Gardening Conference is returning this Saturday to Umpqua Community College. Hosted by the Oregon State University Extension Douglas County Small Farms Program and Douglas County Master Gardener Association, this one-day horticulture event offers a full schedule of educational sessions designed to help participants cultivate healthier, more resilient...

entry.title: FREE VETERAN MENTAL HEALTH DOCUMENTARY SCREENING COMING
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 2:50 a.m. A free public screening of the documentary “HERE.IS.BETTER”. will take place on Saturday, March 21st, starting at 12:00 p.m. at the Roseburg Public Library on Northeast Diamond Lake Boulevard. The event is presented by the Returning Veterans Project, with local support from The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of...

entry.title: Roseburg parents rally after Fremont school staff arrests
entry.summary: Three Fremont Middle School instructors in Roseburg have been arrested and charged after an investigation into student injuries in the Developmental Learning Center, prompting community outrage and parent-led advocacy events for justice and awareness.

entry.title: Restoring Rock Creek: How Viceroy & Skipper Is Rebuilding Glide’s Riparian Future
entry.summary: Hailey Blalock of "Viceroy & Skipper" is restoring Rock Creek in Glide by planting thousands of native trees after the Archie Creek Fire. The effort aims to cool streams for salmon, rebuild habitat, and help Douglas County residents rethink their own landscapes.

entry.title: SUTHERLIN POLICE CHARGE MAN WITH RECENT ROBBERY/BURGLARY AT A BUSINESS
entry.summary: February 28, 2026 9:15 a.m. Sutherlin Police have charged a man following an investigation into a recent robbery at a business. Captain Ben Woodard of SPD said on Sunday, February 15th at 3:30 a.m. officers responded to the Offyce Bar in the 200 block of West Central Avenue after a robbery was reported. Woodward said...

entry.title: Prepare for a total lunar eclipse in March
entry.summary: Twilight briefly beckons a gathering of four planets as March opens. Brilliant Venus is easy to spot low in the west. Next, scan up to the left to spy dimmer Saturn. Quickly sweep with your binoculars to try and find…

entry.title: Gardening activities for March
entry.summary: Question: My daffodils and crocus are blooming. I am so excited about getting my garden started. What are some ideas for early season planting? Answer: With spring just around the corner, it is time to get serious and get your…

author.name: Chris Rusch Master GardnerChris Rusch Master Gardner
